model_narrative: We arrange the contents of a table that has a fixed grid of rows and columns onto a page of limited width. For each row we choose one height out of a list of candidate heights allowed for that row, and from those choices we determine the width given to each cell and to each column. The objective is to minimize the total height of the table.

sets.name: R
sets.doc: the rows of the table, indexed by integers

sets.name: C
sets.doc: the columns of the table, indexed by integers

sets.name: rh
sets.doc: the feasible row-height assignments, given as the set of pairs of a row together with a candidate height it is allowed to take; the second element of each pair is the actual numeric height of the row measured in width-equivalent units, and not every row offers the same list of candidate heights

params.name: pw
params.doc: the page width available for the whole layout, in width units; a single scalar

params.name: minRCHw
params.doc: the minimum width that a given cell requires when its row is set to a particular candidate height, in width units; indexed by a feasible row-height pair together with a column, so it is only defined for the candidate heights that row is allowed to take

vars.name: bRH
vars.doc: selection indicator over feasible row-height pairs; equals 1 if the row is set to that candidate height and 0 otherwise

vars.name: xRCw
vars.doc: the width assigned to each individual cell of the table

vars.name: xCw
vars.doc: the width assigned to each column of the table

vars.name: toth
vars.doc: the total height of the table, in height units; a single scalar

description: Each row of the table must be set to exactly one height. For every row, across all of the candidate heights that row is allowed to take, exactly one of them is selected.

description: The width of each cell is determined by the height chosen for its row. For every cell, its width equals the minimum width that cell requires under whichever candidate height its row ends up taking.

description: Each column must be wide enough to hold every cell in that column. For every cell, the width of its column must be at least the width of that cell.

description: The total height of the table adds up the heights chosen for the rows. Set the total height equal to the sum, over all rows, of the candidate height that each row is set to.

model_narrative: We arrange the contents of a table that has a fixed grid of rows and columns onto a page of limited width. For each row we choose one height out of a list of candidate heights allowed for that row, and from those choices we determine the width given to each cell and to each column. The objective is to minimize the total height of the table.

sets.name: R
sets.doc: the rows of the table, indexed by integers

sets.name: C
sets.doc: the columns of the table, indexed by integers

sets.name: rh
sets.doc: the feasible row-height assignments, given as the set of pairs of a row together with a candidate height it is allowed to take; the second element of each pair is the actual numeric height of the row measured in width-equivalent units, and not every row offers the same list of candidate heights

params.name: pw
params.doc: the page width available for the whole layout, in width units; a single scalar

params.name: minRCHw
params.doc: the minimum width that a given cell requires when its row is set to a particular candidate height, in width units; indexed by a feasible row-height pair together with a column, so it is only defined for the candidate heights that row is allowed to take

vars.name: bRH
vars.doc: selection indicator over feasible row-height pairs; equals 1 if the row is set to that candidate height and 0 otherwise

vars.name: xRCw
vars.doc: the width assigned to each individual cell of the table

vars.name: xCw
vars.doc: the width assigned to each column of the table

vars.name: toth
vars.doc: the total height of the table, in height units; a single scalar

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, each row must be set to exactly one height out of the candidate heights it is allowed to take. Second, the width of every cell is determined by the candidate height chosen for its row, taking the minimum width that cell requires under that height. Third, every column must be at least as wide as each of the cells in that column. Fourth, the total of all the column widths must not exceed the available page width. Finally, the total height of the table is the sum over all rows of the height each row is set to.
